data.title: Chinese Regulators Extend Antitrust Scrutiny of Meituan, Nikkei Asia Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Regulators have extended an antitrust "rectification period" for Chinese food-delivery giant Meituan without giving a clear time frame for when regulatory scrutiny will end, Nikkei Asia reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • Meituan's rectification period, which was supposed to end last October, was extended as the company continued seeking to block restaurants from joining other delivery platforms, Nikkei cites a source as saying.
  • Meituan didn't respond to a request for comment, Nikkei said.

Full story: https://asia.nikkei.com/Business/Technology/Beijing-extends-antitrust-scrutiny-of-food-delivery-leader-Meituan

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: John Wood Group's Finance Chief Resigns
data.description:
John Wood Group (WG.L) said Wednesday that Chief Financial Officer Arvind Balan resigned, effective immediately.

The resignation resulted from discrepancies in the public record regarding his professional certifications, which Balan said should be stated as a certified practicing accountant instead of a chartered accountant.

The British engineering and consulting company will announce a successor and interim CFO at a later date.

data.title: Netwealth Price Target Raised 8% to A$16.20/Share by Barrenjoey
Aristocrat Leisure Price Target Raised 6.3% to A$84.00/Share by Barrenjoey
Goodman Group Completes AU$4 Billion Placement, Shares Down 6%
data.description:
Goodman Group has completed a fully underwritten 'pro-rata' institutional placement to raise about AU$4 billion, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company will issue roughly 119.4 million new securities priced at AU$33.50 each as part of the placement.

The settlement of the placement securities is expected to occur on Feb. 24, with allotment and normal trading occurring on Feb. 25, the filing added.

It also plans to open a security purchase plan with additional information anticipated for release on Thursday, Feb. 27.

Company shares were down 6% in recent trade to reach their lowest since September 2024.

data.title: Alibaba Cloud Launches First Mexican Data Center; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Alibaba Group's cloud unit launched its first data center in Mexico, the company said in a Wednesday statement.

Alibaba Cloud's Mexico cloud region will provide cloud services to businesses, startups, developers and organizations across Latin America.

After the launch, the Chinese tech giant's infrastructure network reached 87 availability zones across 29 regions, the company said.

The company's shares fell less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Pilbara Minerals Price Target Cut 19% to A$2.83/Share by Euroz Hartleys
Australia begins euthanising false killer whales after mass stranding
data.description:
Dozens of false killer whales stranded on a remote beach in the Australian state of Tasmania will be euthanised after bad weather and the isolated location hampered efforts to push them back into the waters, Australian authorities said on Thursday.

More than 150 endangered dolphins, known commonly as false killer whales due to their resemblance to killer whales, were found stranded this week in the island state's northwest coast near Arthur River, about 400 km (250 miles) from Tasmania's state capital, Hobart.

"The conditions that the team faced yesterday in attempts to refloat the whales proved very challenging and in fact, dangerous to our staff," Tasmania Parks and Wildlife Service Brendon Clark said in a media briefing.

A total of 27 animals were euthanised on Thursday morning while 38 were still alive, Clark said.

The euthanasia process is expected to finish later on Thursday.

Officials have said the stranding response would be complex due to the inaccessibility of the site, ocean conditions and the challenges of getting specialist equipment to the remote area.

The animals can grow up to 20 feet (6.1 m) long and weigh up to 3,000 lbs (1,361 kg). They are found in all tropical and subtropical oceans, generally in deep offshore waters, according to the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

data.title: Why Ghost Judge Should Be Your Next Must-Read
data.description:
LAS VEGAS, NV / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Legal thrillers have long captivated readers with their intricate plots and high-stakes drama, but Regina Keelan Bass's Ghost Judge takes the genre to unprecedented heights. This debut novel deftly combines the procedural intricacies of courtroom battles with the chilling allure of the supernatural, creating a narrative that is as unique as it is compelling.

For anyone who craves a story that challenges the mind while tugging at the heart, Ghost Judge is a must-read. At its core, the novel tells the story of Judge Katelyn Mundy, a determined jurist who becomes entangled in a murder trial that hits startlingly close to home. Mike McDavid, a hotheaded lawyer with a history of clashing with her deceased friend and mentor, Judge David Cover, stands accused of Cover's murder.

However, the trial takes an unexpected turn when Cover's ghost appears to Mundy, revealing McDavid's innocence and urging her to uncover the true perpetrator. This revelation sets off a chain of events that forces Mundy to confront her deepest fears and question the very foundations of her beliefs.

What makes Ghost Judge stand out is its ability to seamlessly weave together multiple narrative layers. On one level, it is a gripping mystery, complete with twists and turns that keep readers guessing.

Yet, on another level, it is an exploration of ethical dilemmas, examining the challenges faced by those tasked with upholding justice in an imperfect system. Bass's nuanced portrayal of the legal profession adds a layer of authenticity that elevates the story beyond mere fiction.

Equally compelling are the novel's characters, who are as complex as they are relatable. Judge Mundy is a particularly captivating protagonist, embodying a blend of strength, vulnerability, and moral clarity that makes her journey deeply resonant. Her interactions with the spectral Judge Cover are both poignant and thought-provoking, offering insights into the enduring bonds of friendship and the quest for closure. Meanwhile, McDavid's volatile personality and turbulent past add an element of unpredictability, ensuring that the narrative remains dynamic and engaging.

Moreover, the novel's richly detailed setting enhances its impact. Bass's evocative descriptions of Kansas City's landmarks, from the historic Truman Courthouse to the vibrant Green Lady Lounge, immerse readers in a world that feels both familiar and otherworldly. These locations serve as more than mere backdrops; they become integral to the story, reflecting its themes of preservation, memory, and legacy.

In addition to its engrossing narrative, Ghost Judge offers a fresh perspective on the mystery genre. While many legal thrillers focus solely on the mechanics of the courtroom, Bass expands the scope to include the human and supernatural elements that influence justice.

This approach not only broadens the novel's appeal but also invites readers to reflect on the deeper questions it poses: What constitutes justice? How do personal biases shape our perceptions of guilt and innocence? And what happens when the boundaries between the natural and the supernatural begin to blur?

Furthermore, Bass's writing is as precise as it is evocative. Her background in observing legal nuances lends credibility to the procedural elements, while her flair for storytelling ensures that the narrative remains engaging. Additionally, her ability to balance suspense with introspection results in a story that is both thrilling and profoundly moving.

For readers who value authenticity, Ghost Judge delivers in spades. The novel's inspiration, drawn from Bass's own experiences and insights, lends it a depth and realism that few fictional works achieve.

The characters, too, are grounded in reality, many of them inspired by individuals from Bass's personal and professional life. This connection between fiction and reality enriches the story, allowing it to resonate on multiple levels.

Ultimately, Ghost Judge is more than just a mystery; it is a meditation on justice, friendship, and the enduring power of truth. It challenges readers to think critically while offering an emotional journey that lingers long after the final page.

Whether you are a seasoned fan of legal thrillers or a newcomer to the genre, this novel promises an experience unlike any other. Regina Keelan Bass's Ghost Judge is a triumph of storytelling, marking the arrival of a fresh and distinctive voice in contemporary fiction. Don't miss the chance to discover this extraordinary tale-a book that truly has it all.

﻿﻿Name: Regina Bass

Email reginabass@reginakeelanbass.co.site

Organisation: Regina's Writes

City, State, Contact: Las Vegas NV

Contact Number: (702) 764-3296

SOURCE: Regina's Writes

data.title: Federal Reserve speakers Thursday include Kugler, Goolsbee, Musalem, Barr
data.description:
The times below are in GMT/US Eastern time format:

1435/0935 Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee participates in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Chicagoland Chamber Mid-Market Chicago event

1705/1205 Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem speaks before the Economic Club of New York

1930/1430 Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michael Barr speaks on "Supervision and Regulation" at the Georgetown University Law Center

2200/1700 Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler speaks on "Navigating Inflation Waves While Riding on the Phillips Curve" before the 2025 Whittington Lecture event hosted by Georgetown University

All of these except perhaps Barr look like they are likely to contain relevant remarks for traders.

Earlier in the session here we had Vice Chair Jefferson speaking. he sounded very happy to sit on hold for a while to come:

  • Fed's Jefferson says the Bank can take its time to consider its next monetary policy move
  • Fed's Jefferson says Fed is in "watch and wait" mode about state of government policy

data.title: Google Pixel-maker Dixon to double revenue as India ramps up electronics production
data.description:
Dixon Technologies India , which assembles Google's Pixel smartphones, is set to more than double its revenue this fiscal year, driven by rapid growth in local electronics manufacturing, a top executive told Reuters.

Electronics manufacturing has gained momentum in India as global giants, including Alphabet's Google and Apple , expand their supply chain away from China.

"(For) the sector and Dixon, the growth path is going to be extremely aggressive in the coming future," Managing Director Atul Lall said on Wednesday.

The contract manufacturer reported a revenue of 177.13 billion rupees ($2.04 billion) for the 2024 financial year that ended in March, up 45% from a year earlier. Its revenue stood at 285.77 billion rupees for the nine months ended December 31.

Noida-based Dixon, which also assembles smartphones for firms such as China's Xiaomi and Oppo, has branched out into component manufacturing as India plans to offer billions of dollars in incentives to make parts for mobiles and laptops.

India's electronics manufacturing sector is set to grow to 6 trillion rupees in fiscal year 2027, from 1.46 trillion rupees in 2022, brokerage Motilal Oswal said in a note in December.

However, U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to impose reciprocal tariffs from early April could pose a risk, with analysts estimating potential losses at about $7 billion a year for India's export sectors.

Lall said Dixon, which has invested heavily to cater to rising export demand for electronics, is awaiting more details on the issue as the U.S. has made only broader statements so far.

($1 = 86.8580 Indian rupees)

data.title: China Minsheng Banking Raises 10 Billion Yuan Via Green Bond Issue
data.description:
China Minsheng Banking raised 10 billion yuan through the issue of its first tranche of 2025 green financial bonds.

The three-year bonds, which carry a 1.7% coupon rate, were issued Tuesday, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Proceeds will be used to fund green industry projects in line with the Green Bond Support Project Catalog.

data.title: Most metals climb as Trump signals new trade deal with China
data.description:
Most metals rose as U.S. President Donald Trump signalled a new trade deal with top consumer China, while aluminium held steady at a one-month high on Thursday after EU envoys' agreement to ban Russian primary aluminium imports in the sanctions package.

Trump indicated that a new trade deal with China is possible, noting that the U.S. once had a strong trade agreement with China and highlighting his positive relationship with President Xi Jinping.

Three-month aluminium on the London Metals Exchange(LME) was up 0.6% at $2,703.5 a ton, as of 0417 GMT, its highest point since January 20.

The Russian aluminium import ban will be phased in a year from the official adoption, EU diplomats said.

The longer-term effect on metals from the geopolitical front was unclear. U.S. President Donald Trump's administration agreed on Tuesday to hold more talks with Russia on ending the war in Ukraine.

Russia is a major producer of aluminium, nickel and copper. The LME banned supplies of Russian metals made after April 2024 to its system last year.

Meanwhile, sources told Reuters that the Trump administration may seek to strike a simplified minerals deal with Ukraine to get a pact in place quickly and later negotiate detailed terms, such as how much of Ukraine's vast resources the U.S. would own.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) CMCU3 edged 0.5% higher to $9,512 a metric ton.

Zinc added 0.4% to $2,888, tin gained 0.6% to $32,895, lead rose 0.2% to $2,001 and nickel gained 0.8% to $15,530.

SHFE aluminium gained 0.6% to 20,805 yuan ($2,862.63)a ton, SHFE copper rose 0.4% to 77,330 yuan, nickel climbed 0.5% to 124,390 yuan, while zinc lost 0.1% to 23,950 yuan, lead rose 0.2% to 17,065 yuan and tin gained 0.3% to 262,280 yuan.

($1 = 7.2678 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Rio Tinto Board Urges Shareholders to Vote Against Review of Dual Listing Structure
data.description:
Rio Tinto Group's board unanimously urged shareholders to vote against a resolution calling for an independent review of the mining giant's dual listing in Australia and the UK.

The company said Wednesday the resolution requisitioned by certain shareholders for the April 3 annual general meeting is against the best interests of shareholders and Rio Tinto as a whole.

Rio Tinto noted that a unified Australian listing suggested by the resolution would be "value destructive" for the company with higher tax costs.

The resolution was sought by activist investor Palliser Capital along with more than 100 other shareholders, Reuters noted in a report on Wednesday.

data.title: UAE's Palm Sports to Provide Multi-martial Arts Training Services to Government Institution
data.description:
International Holding (ADX: IHC) subsidiary Palms Sports (ADX:PALMS) secured a new deal worth 40 million Emirati dirhams to provide multi-martial arts training services to an undisclosed government entity.

Under the two-year contract, the Abu Dhabi-based sports management company will assign instructors to meet the requirements of the institution's workforce, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Shanghai Electric Power Raises 3.8 Billion Yuan to Repay Debts, Loans
data.description:
Shanghai Electric Power raised 3.8 billion yuan via the issue of its first and second ultra-short-term financing bonds for 2025.

Proceeds will mainly be used to repay maturing debts and settle financial institution loans, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The 86-day bond raised 1.7 billion yuan, while the 149-day bond raised 2.1 billion yuan. Interest rates were 1.98% and 1.96%, respectively.

Shares of the power generation company were down less than 1% in recent trade.

data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.4% at 75,640, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.3% to 22,865, tracking their Asian peers, as uncertainty over U.S. tariff measures dented investor sentiment.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose about 0.2% to 86.8050 per U.S. dollar, tracking a rise in the Chinese yuan and other regional peers, after U.S. President Donald Trump said a new trade deal with Beijing was possible.

data.title: CECEP Solar Energy's Units to Trim Electricity Settlement Price of Photovoltaic Project
data.description:
CECEP Solar Energy's units agreed to lower the settlement price for the operation of a rooftop distributed photovoltaic power generation project, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

CEC Hubei Solar Technology had agreed to develop CEC (Jingmen) Environmental Science Water Technology Development's 2-megawatt rooftop photovoltaic power project in 2024.

In the amended deal, the settlement electricity price is no longer 0.68 yuan per kilowatt hour but will be lowered to 0.5 yuan/kWh, which is based on CEC Jingmen's quarterly electricity bill, the solar panel manufacturer said.

data.title: Rising costs, weak demand hit small, midcap Q3 earnings
data.description:
After outperforming large caps for most of FY24, Small and MidCap profits have now started to lag behind for the second consecutive quarter. This underperformance is largely attributed to a domestic slowdown, to which small and midcaps are more exposed, experts said.

According to Moneycontrol data, an aggregate of 683 firms in the BSE SmallCap Index reported a 7.1 percent increase in revenue for Q3, marking the seventh consecutive quarter of single-digit growth and the 13th straight quarter of sequential single-digit growth.

However, a sharp rise in the cost of finished goods purchases and employee expenses, both growing in double digits for the second consecutive quarter, has impacted profitability. Net profit for the December quarter recorded a modest 1.5 percent year-on-year increase, the slowest growth in six quarters, while sequentially, net profit declined for the second consecutive quarter. Only those firms that had comparable data for 15 previous quarters were chosen.

For the BSE MidCap Index, Moneycontrol has aggregate data for 85 firms. Despite a 12.9 percent rise in revenue for Q3, the highest in eight quarters, net profit increased by only 2 percent, marking the slowest growth in six quarters. The subdued profit growth, despite higher revenue, was attributed to rising total expenses and interest costs. Total costs surged by 11 percent, the steepest increase in six quarters, while interest costs soared over 27 percent, the highest jump in three quarters.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

Energy, banking and financial services companies were excluded from the study.

The weak earnings in the mid-cap and small-cap space can be attributed to poor demand, and inflationary pressures which directly affected consumer spending. Also, global uncertainties, rising input costs, and higher borrowing costs strained the bottom line figures. The growing premiumization trend has also affected smaller companies which are still at the nascent stage as compared to large counterparts, experts said.

Mayank Mundhra, FRM- VP Risk & Head Research Abans Group said weaker-than-expected growth—driven by sluggish global demand from key trade partners like the US and Europe—led to margin compression. This slowdown was further intensified by the 2024 election cycle, as markets anticipated potential shifts in political power globally, leading to cautious spending and weaker demand.

Sectors like capital goods, industrials, and consumer discretionary were hit hardest, struggling with weak demand and high capital needs. Rising borrowing costs further pressured profits despite revenue growth.

Experts say small and midcap margins are lower and more cyclical than large caps due to their growth focus. Post-COVID, they saw stronger margin gains, but with tailwinds fading, pressure is mounting. While consensus expects SMID profits to outperform in FY26, weak revenue and shrinking margins put this at risk. Continued underperformance could threaten their valuation premiums.

Motilal Oswal’s latest note highlights that the recent correction in broader markets has accounted for some potential earnings disappointments ahead. However, mid and small-cap valuations remain elevated compared to their historical averages and relative to the Nifty 50. The Nifty is currently trading at a 12-month forward P/E of 19.3x, below its long-term average of 20.5x. Given this backdrop, the brokerage maintains a preference for large-cap stocks.

Narinder Wadhwa, Managing Director & CEO of SKI capital services said key risks persist, including double-digit growth in employee costs, which could offset potential cost savings, and a weak demand environment that may constrain earnings growth. Additionally, global economic factors such as tariff wars and the likelihood of no Fed rate cuts further add to the uncertainty.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: BASF Plans New Mid-double-digit Million-euro Alcoholates Plant in Germany
data.description:
BASF disclosed on Wednesday that it is investing a mid-double-digit million-euro amount for the development of a new alcoholates plant at its site in Ludwigshafen, Germany.

The new plant will replace the German chemicals group's existing production facility in the city, with startup expected in the second half of 2027.

The plant will produce sodium methylate and potassium methylate, which are used for biodiesel production, as well as for pharmaceutical and agricultural applications.

data.title: Lenovo Doubles Profit on Strong Sales
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Lenovo Group reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, with device sales strong, boosted by its bet on the fast-growing use of artificial intelligence.

Net profit for the three months ended December more than doubled to $693 million, the world's largest PC maker said Thursday, beating the $381.7 million estimate in a FactSet poll of analysts. The result was partly boosted by a $282 million nonrecurring income tax credit.

Revenue was $18.80 billion, up 20% from a year earlier, also topping analysts' estimates. Lenovo's main business, the intelligent-devices segment, which includes PCs, smartphones and tablets, delivered a 12% revenue gain.

The results reflect a solid commercial PC replacement cycle but also show that the company is reaping the benefits after launching several AI PC models in China last May.

Lenovo Chief Executive Yang Yuanqing told The Wall Street Journal in November that AI PCs could make up 25% of its global PC shipments this year and reach 70%-80% by 2027 as the premium product becomes mainstream.

The company's Hong Kong-listed shares have risen more than 25% so far this year, driven in part by investors' exuberance over Chinese AI startup DeepSeek's breakthrough in January.

Lenovo has been touted as a key beneficiary of the tech advancement. Analysts at UOB said that cheaper, more accessible AI features introduced by DeepSeek could encourage more enterprises, especially small and midsize companies, to invest in AI equipment, which may accelerate replacement demand for PCs and servers.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com
